Front page Local Sports Obituaries

City Council Approves Riverside Park Renovation

The city council voted seven to two on Tuesday night to approve the long-debated renovation of Riverside Park, ending a planning process that stretched across three administrations and two public referendums. Work is scheduled to begin in September and continue through the following autumn.

The approved plan replaces the deteriorating boathouse with a smaller community pavilion, restores the original 1920s bandshell, and extends the riverfront path north to the rail bridge. A separate measure funding additional lighting along the path passed unanimously after a brief discussion of maintenance costs.

Council members who opposed the package cited the loss of forty parking spaces near the boat launch. Supporters countered that the new shuttle agreement with the transit authority more than offsets the loss on summer weekends, pointing to ridership data from the pilot program that ran last July.

Funding combines a state recreation grant with the parks levy approved by voters two years ago. The city manager confirmed that no general-fund money is committed to the project, and that the contingency reserve survives intact even under the most pessimistic construction estimate presented during hearings.
